NVDA.reasoning: No valid breakout setup is present. The relevant range boundaries are: upper consolidation resistance at $208.20 (former 15-test support, now resistance per wiki; confirmed breach on April 30 with 1.52x avg volume = bearish breakout already completed) and base support at $197.12 (recent low). The downside breakout from the $208.20 level has already fired and partially exhausted — price dropped from $216.83 to $198.45 (-8.5%) in 4 sessions. The breakout is done; it is not my setup anymore. For a new bullish breakout opportunity, I require a confirmed daily close above $203.00 (hourly pivot, 12-test volume-confirmed resistance) with volume 1.5x+ the 20-day average (current: 0.87x — no confirmation). Bollinger Band width on the daily is 0.2224 (not compressed; volatility already expanded). Hourly BB width is 0.1123, tighter but with price near the lower band (pct_b 0.17) and hourly RSI-21 at 18.82 — deeply oversold but no bullish reversal candle yet. Squeeze status: no active squeeze; the hourly expansion already fired bearishly (MACD histogram -1.82, -DI 36.05 >> +DI 12.97). Wiki confirms both stops were hit at $205.30 on April 30; prior lesson flags a false breakout pattern on NVDA (EMA pullback failed, -$63.20). The $208.20 level is now overhead resistance. Price may be forming a base at $197-$199, consistent with the wiki's target entry zone ($197.50-$199.50), but a base is not a breakout — I need to see the coil complete and a confirmed volume surge above resistance before acting. No entry until close above $203.00 on 1.5x+ volume.
